It was common in the Jewish nation, that among the Jews they went by a Jewish name; but among heathens by an- other. That is, either by the same name turned into the heathen language; as Tabitha to the Jews was Dorcas to them that spake Greek; and Thomas to the Hebrews was Didymus to the Greeks; and perhaps Silas to the Jews was Tertius to the Romans, Rom. xvi. 22, from wibw Shalosh, three ; and Jason was Secundus: compare Rom. xvi. with Acts xx.4. Or they went by some different name; as Herod in Luke, Acts xii.i, is Agrippa in Josephus ; and John is also Mark, Acts xii. 12.
